sourceFile: hand-authored synthetic fixture (no kglw.net source row) -- generated via a small Node script, not hand-typed, to avoid transcription errors; see packages/core/test/eval/backtest.test.ts for how it is normalized and consumed
why:
Hand-designed for Plan 02-04's holdout + walk-forward backtest tests (D-12/D-13, RESEARCH M5/M7, Pitfall 3). Two tours: 'Synthetic Legacy Tour' (tourId 999, dated 2024-05-18..20) and 'Synthetic Holdout Tour' (tourId 7, dated 2024-06-01..03). tourId 999 is the MAX tourId in the corpus but is chronologically EARLIEST -- a max(tourId) holdout bug would incorrectly pick tourId 999 instead of the true holdout (tourId 7, the tour containing the latest-dated show). findHoldoutShows must return exactly the 3 tourId-7 shows.

Training data (the legacy tour) establishes an EXCLUSIVE (t1=1.0) single successor for every chain song -- P(801)->Q(802)->R(803)->U(804)->T(806)->W(807)->X(808)->Y(809)->Z(810), plus Y->Z repeated 3 times across all 3 legacy shows, every instance notated as a segue (transition_id 2), and Z->AA(811) once (free-choice, transition_id 1 on Z's row). This gives Y->Z a segueRate of 3/3 = 1.0 >= hardSegueConsistencyThreshold (0.70) with totalExits(Y) = 3 >= hardSegueMinSupport (3) -- clears the D-04 gate and PINS to hardSegueOverrideCeiling (0.97) regardless of the ordinary multiplicative pipeline, per predict.ts's applyOverride. Because every OTHER chain song has exactly one exclusive training successor (t1=1.0, weight 0.6 of the backoff blend) while every competitor candidate's t1 contribution from that same A is exactly 0 (bounded above by the remaining w2+w3+w4=0.4 tiers spread across the whole candidate universe), the correct next song is the dominant-probability winner for every ordinary (non-pinned) eval transition too -- verified against the actual implementation, not asserted blindly, since the exact interpolated-blend/eraPrior arithmetic is not practical to fully hand-derive; the DESIGN INTENT (single exclusive successor per chain link, small catalog, dates close together so recency decay is ~1.0 for all edges, and uniform-ish playCounts so eraPrior stays a near-uniform multiplier that cannot overturn a 0.6-vs-0.4 backoff margin) is what a human reviewer can verify by inspection, and `npx vitest run` confirms the deterministic pure functions agree.

Holdout tour eval transitions (walked in (date, showOrder) order):
- H1 Set1 [P,Q,R]: eval P->Q, Q->R (2 free-choice transitions, exclusive-trained). Set2 [U] alone: single-song set, 0 eval transitions (D-13/M7 exclusion).
- H2 Set1 [T, Unknown(song_id 1)]: T->placeholder is EXCLUDED because B is the sentinel/placeholder song (M7 exclusion) -- 0 net eval transitions from this pair. Set2 [W,X]: eval W->X (1 free-choice transition, exclusive-trained).
- H3 Set1 [Y,Z,AA]: eval Y->Z (1 HARD-SEGUE transition -- A=Y has transitionKind 'segue' on its own row -- gated PIN per above) and Z->AA (1 free-choice transition, exclusive-trained).

Hand-computed expected values: evalTransitionCount = 5 (2 + 0 + 1 + 1 + 1; the raw adjacent-pair count before the placeholder exclusion would be 6, proving the exclusion actually fires). hardSegue.n = 1 (Y->Z only), freeChoice.n = 4 (P->Q, Q->R, W->X, Z->AA); hardSegue.n + freeChoice.n = overall.n = 5. Every one of the 5 eval transitions is designed to be the dominant/pinned top-1 candidate (overall.top1 = overall.top5 = overall.top10 = 5/5), because (a) the catalog is small (10 non-placeholder songs total: P,Q,R,U,T,W,X,Y,Z,AA) so predict's default candidateListSize (15) always returns the full candidate universe, guaranteeing top5/top10 = 100% trivially, and (b) every correct B has either an exclusive t1=1.0 training edge or the Y->Z hard-segue pin, guaranteeing top1 too -- see the code comment above for the margin argument.

No-leakage check (M5): building the matrix with the SAME exclusive (date, showOrder) bound the backtest uses for H1 (date 2024-06-01, showOrder 1, inclusive: false) must show the P->Q edge with count exactly 1 (from OLD-Show1 only) -- if H1's own P->Q occurrence leaked into its own training matrix, the count would be 2. This directly exercises D-12's 'train = everything strictly prior' invariant on the first held-out show, where there is no prior holdout-tour night to obscure the check.
